items.label: wait
items.leak_reason: No WaitGroup, context, or shutdown drain exists; the TODO comment explicitly acknowledges goroutines leak until OS cleanup on server exit.
items.llm_summary: One goroutine is spawned per started job and runs until the child process exits, but there is no mechanism to signal or join these goroutines on server shutdown, causing leaks for any in-flight jobs at exit time.
items.source_snippet:
// jobworker/manager.go:31-70
  31 | func (jm *JobManager) Start(command string, owner string, args ...string) (string, error) {
  32 | 
  33 | 	id := generateID()
  34 | 
  35 | 	cmd := exec.Command(command, args...) //nolint:gosec // arbitrary command running is by design
  36 | 	ob := NewOutputBuffer()
  37 | 	cmd.Stderr = ob
  38 | 	cmd.Stdout = ob
  39 | 
  40 | 	// if it doesnt start we dont have an id to return
  41 | 	if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {
  42 | 		log.Printf("Start: failed to start job with command %s: %v", command, err)
  43 | 		return "", err
  44 | 	}
  45 | 
  46 | 	job := &Job{
  47 | 		Info: JobInfo{
  48 | 			ID:     id,
  49 | 			Owner:  owner,
  50 | 			Status: JobStatusRunning,
  51 | 		},
  52 | 		Cmd:    cmd,
  53 | 		output: ob,
  54 | 	}
  55 | 
  56 | 	// lock to append the job's particulars and then unlock
  57 | 	jm.mu.Lock()
  58 | 	jm.jobs[id] = job
  59 | 	jm.mu.Unlock()
  60 | 
  61 | 	// spawn the Waiter
  62 | 	// TODO: add a Shutdown() to drain wait goroutines on teardown.
  63 | 	// without it, goroutines leak on server exit (until OS cleanup)
  64 | 	// an implementation could be: track goroutines with a WaitGroup, kill all running jobs
  65 | 	// and then call Wait()
  66 | 	go jm.wait(job)
  67 | 
  68 | 	return id, nil
  69 | 
  70 | }

items.label: JobManager.Output/anon#2
items.leak_reason: If the caller never cancels the context and never reads the buffer to drain it, the goroutine blocks indefinitely; the comment warns callers but there is no enforced timeout or WaitGroup tracking.
items.llm_summary: A per-request watcher goroutine that closes an OutputBufferReader when either the caller's context is cancelled or the buffer drains, with a medium leak risk if callers ignore both signals.
